title: Design skill-first Workflow Foundry revamp
summary: Design a complete skill-first revamp of the Workflow Foundry so requests to create skills become explicit callable skill-package contracts, use approved workflow capabilities when needed, and cannot be satisfied by Python, JavaScript, or helper-script substitutes. Trace the current routing and validation failure, define the target architecture and migration plan, and preserve Matt Pocock and ECC phase gates before implementation begins.
acceptance_criteria: grilling maps the complete request path from user intent through project selection, task draft, capability dependency selection, Matt phase work, approved artifacts, validation, review, and handoff
the revamp defines precise foundry terms for a canonical repo skill, a project-local packaged skill, a workflow artifact, and a deterministic helper so scripts cannot be mistaken for skills
task creation and continuation classify the requested deliverable before implementation instead of representing every request as an untyped workflow-change

a task classified as a canonical skill requires a lowercase hyphenated .agents/skills/<slug>/ folder with SKILL.md and agents/openai.yaml; optional scripts, references, and assets remain supporting resources rather than the product
a task classified as a project-local packaged skill names its product boundary and required skill bundle without silently promoting it into the active repo-local .agents/skills surface

Python remains forbidden in this repository; repo-local .mjs remains limited to deterministic JSON validation or query support; existing approved UI or package code stays within its explicit boundary; none of these can satisfy a skill deliverable by themselves
workflow-help exposes an explicit skill-construction route instead of leaving create-skill requests to generic global authoring guidance with conflicting destination roots
capability dependencies influence the required output contract and record provenance; shape-valid dependency metadata alone does not count as a successful skill outcome

validation fails missing SKILL.md, missing or invalid agents/openai.yaml, folder/frontmatter mismatch, script-only skill substitutes, unreferenced helpers, command-first wrappers, and workflow-help discovery drift
eval fixtures cover natural-language create-skill requests and prove the foundry chooses the correct skill surface before any code or helper artifact is approved
the migration preserves the existing task tracker, phase guards, audit separation, read-only tracker UI, source-project isolation, and approval boundary for external writes
the workflow preserves Matt Pocock presentation style and ECC workflow architecture
the workflow produces a reviewable target architecture and migration roadmap before any implementation artifacts are approved

context_snapshot.summary: Load workflow-foundry state, all non-done tasks, the prior skill-first repair, current routing and validator surfaces, and Matt/ECC skill guidance before shaping the skill-first foundry revamp.

session_log.event: Created intake task for a foundry expansion discovery workflow.
Moved to grilling after user asked to grill the foundry expansion discovery workflow.

Resolved operator job: inspect current foundry repo state across projects, skills, validators, trackers, and gaps, then produce a ranked shortlist of candidate workflow additions with evidence, expected value, and the next tracker task to create.

User resumed the task and identified the primary revamp failure: create-skill requests still drift into Python, JavaScript, or helper-script outputs instead of producing actual callable skills governed by the workflow dependency model and ECC.

Completed an evidence-first architecture trace. Root policy is skill-first, but workflow-help has no skill-construction route; initiate-task and continue-task model skill intent as a generic workflow-change; the validator checks existing SKILL.md files but does not require a complete skill bundle, agents/openai.yaml, deliverable-to-output consistency, dependency outcome evidence, or discovery reconciliation.

Reframed workflow-foundry-006 from general expansion discovery into the skill-first foundry revamp owner. Kept the task in grilling with no approved implementation artifacts while the target-surface routing rule remains unresolved.

User instructed the task to continue through PRD, accepting the recommended routing law: foundry/control-plane skills target the canonical repo skill surface, standalone product skills remain in their product repository, ambiguous destinations stop for clarification, and scripts may support but never replace a requested skill. Approved the project-local PRD artifact only; no implementation artifacts were approved.

Closed grilling with a versioned multi-deliverable contract model, three-way skill ownership routing, one dedicated model-invoked skill builder, thin lifecycle delegation, positive bundle and completion enforcement, capability outcome reconciliation, derived skill discovery, and hybrid routing plus disposable-repository regression seams.

Wrote the skill-first Workflow Foundry revamp PRD, moved the task to PRD phase, and set issue breakdown as the next action. No implementation artifacts, dependency calls, commits, or pushes were approved.

Completed a focused PRD review and tightened three contracts before handoff: legacy open tasks are phase-frozen until explicit versioned migration, behavioral eval evidence records raw scenarios and observed outcomes, and $build-workflow-skill is the stable model-invoked authoring authority.

User instructed the task to continue through issue breakdown and explicitly approved pushing the resulting checkpoint. The checkpoint remains scoped to workflow-foundry-006 and must exclude unrelated dirty project and tracker work.

Converted the approved PRD into seven blocker-ordered slices: one validation-core prefactor followed by end-to-end deliverable migration, derived discovery, canonical skill construction, standalone routing, multi-deliverable routing, and capability outcome reconciliation. Every routing slice includes deterministic and fresh-agent evidence, tracker schema writes remain owned by workflow-foundry-004, and no implementation artifact is approved.

Completed focused issue review and resolved all blockers: Issue 002 now requires workflow-foundry-004 tracker authorization and complete contract identity fields; Issue 005 covers both in-foundry packaged and standalone product routes; Issues 004 through 007 share one live-plus-deterministic scenario contract; and command-first regression criteria explicitly cover Quick Start, Getting Started, Example, and unheaded loopholes.

Validated the exact scoped staged snapshot in an isolated worktree. Root workflow-state validation passed; tracker verification passed eight data tests, the Astro production build, and one generated-output test; structural checks confirmed seven issues, all 44 PRD stories, resolvable blocker references, and no implementation artifact approval.

Created scoped checkpoint c3098f8 (docs: plan skill-first foundry revamp) and pushed it to origin/main. The commit contains only the workflow-foundry-006 PRD, seven issue artifacts, task index entry, and task state; unrelated Health, LinkedIn, workflow-foundry-014, and other dirty tracker changes remained outside the checkpoint.
